source_jobs.title: Sr. Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.





ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Sr. Product Designer to help shape how enterprises run on Console. Console resolves back-office tasks as they come in from Slack or Teams. Instead of handling requests manually, teams configure workflows, define policies, and rely on AI agents to take action. You’ll help make these systems intuitive and reliable.



You’ll work closely with founders and customers to turn real workflows into simple, high-quality product experiences. This is a high-ownership role. You’ll drive work end-to-end, make product decisions, and operate with a high degree of autonomy.




ABOUT YOU

 - You have 6+ years of product design experience, ideally at early stage startups where you are resource-constrained

 - You have an exceptionally high bar for craft and quality. Strong IA and UX design thinking is evident in portfolio and past work

 - You have full mastery of Figma

 - You have experience (or willingness) to work across the full product lifecycle - navigating ambiguity and customer requests, converting them into product requirements, and ultimately into shippable designs

Willingness to do non-product design work when needed (rarely): brand tweaks, slides, website tweaks, office design, etc. (You will own all design, even if you are not the one executing it specifically)

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits including:
   
   - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
   
   - Unlimited PTO
   
   - 401(k)
   
   - Equity
   
   - Meals provided daily in office

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring an Applied AI Engineer as the number and complexity of AI workflows in our system scale rapidly. As more customers rely on Console to automate critical back-office operations, we need engineers who can push our core agent loop to be faster, smarter, and more reliable.



You’ll own and evolve the core agent loop and the AI workflows that make the product feel like magic. Working closely with product and leadership, you’ll ship fast, build intuitive and resilient systems, and drive projects end-to-end, helping shape both the platform and the future of the product.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You’ll build and improve AI systems that operate in real-world conditions, where reliability, speed, and adaptability matter. At Console, you will:

 - Build intelligent agents that automate work and significantly increase productivity

 - Design copilot-style workflows that help human operators do more, faster

 - Treat agent quality like a first-class ML optimization problem using real usage data to make the core agent loop to be more reliable, adaptable, and resilient

 - Build category defining product capabilities, from idea to production




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a product-minded engineer who enjoys building real systems people depend on. You’ll likely have:

 - 4+ years of software engineering experience, with a focus on applied AI products

 - Proven track record of shipping agentic systems used by real users

 - Comfortable across the stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) and debugging production systems

 - Strong product sense, with a focus on building intuitive, reliable systems

 - Ownership mindset: you drive projects end-to-end and iterate quickly from real usage

 - Enjoy following SOTA research into new models and agent architectures

 - Comfortable building products that live at the intersection of AI and ML

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits including:
   
   - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
   
   - Unlimited PTO
   
   - 401(k)
   
   - Equity
   
   - Meals provided daily in office

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.





ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Product Engineer as the product continues to expand and more teams rely on Console to run internal operations. You’ll work right at the boundary between our AI agents and the product, shaping how agent capabilities turn into real workflows that actually get work done.



You’ll take ideas from concept to production, own them end-to-end, and define how work is structured, executed, and improved across the platform as we scale. You'll have room to be creative, own projects end-to-end, and shape both the platform and the product vision.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design workflow primitives and capabilities that define how work gets created, tracked, and completed

 - Build AI-powered product flows, making outputs usable, predictable, and reliable

 - Define how users interact with and manage workflows, from initiation to completion

 - Create feedback loops in the product so workflows improve from real usage

 - Handle messy, real-world inputs and ensure systems behave consistently

 - Expand the platform with new workflows, integrations, and ways to get work done

 - Turn ambiguous ideas into shipped features with product and leadership




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a product-minded engineer who likes figuring out how systems should behave, not just implementing them. You’ll likely have:

 - 4+ years of full-time experience in a product focused software engineering role

 - Comfort working across the stack (TypeScript / Node.js / React)

 - Experience building and shipping product features or systems end-to-end

 - Strong product instincts and a focus on how things actually work for users

 - Ability to take something vague and turn it into something real

 - Comfort working with real-world systems that don’t behave perfectly every time

 - A sense of ownership and a bias toward moving quickly and iterating from real usage

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits including:
   
   - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
   
   - Unlimited PTO
   
   - 401(k)
   
   - Equity
   
   - Meals provided daily in office

source_jobs.title: Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.


THE ROLE

As a Design Engineer at Console, you’ll be responsible for the touches that make our product feel like magic. You will work closely with the product and design, and build out the front-end infrastructure that will separate Console from our competitors.

Some examples of work you might do:

 - Build a smooth and performant agent UX with streaming responses and interleaved UI components

 - Design, extend, and own Console's design system

 - Find screens, views, and components that could use a bit of animation flair, then dial it in

You'll straddle the boundary of design and engineering, applying your own taste and creativity and helping bring the product vision to life in beautiful detail.




ABOUT YOU

 - You have strong generalist design and technical skills, and you know how to maximize your impact to make the product feel great and work well

 - You have excellent taste; you understand that the best product experiences feel great to use and you care deeply about getting the little details right

 - You have experience driving all the way from idea -> design -> implementation

 - You have a strong front-end skillset, have worked with and built out design systems, and are comfortable collaborating closely with other designers




REQUIREMENTS

 - 4+ years of full-time design or design engineering experience, and a strong grasp on front-end engineering

 - Comfortable working and contributing across the entire stack (Typescript/Node/React)

 - Obsessed with building incredible product experiences for users, and never happy with "good enough"

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits including:
   
   - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
   
   - Unlimited PTO
   
   - 401(k)
   
   - Equity
   
   - Meals provided daily in office

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.






ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Enterprise FDE at Console, you’ll be the technical owner for our largest and most complex customer deployments. You’ll work directly with customer teams to understand their environment, design the right implementation path, and solve the technical problems that determine whether a deployment succeeds.

You’ll work in complex customer environments where the right answer is rarely obvious from the start. In partnership with Deployment Strategists, you’ll help turn customer goals into implementation plans that are grounded in technical reality and built to survive enterprise constraints.

This is a highly technical, highly customer-facing role for someone who wants to build in the field. You’ll configure complex AI workflows, debug issues across customer environments, ship product changes or workarounds when needed, and bring the patterns you see back into the platform. You’ll have room to operate autonomously, work with some of the world’s largest companies, and shape how enterprise teams deploy AI in production.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the technical success of enterprise deployments, from evaluation through implementation, launch, and expansion

 - Understand customer environments deeply: systems, integrations, identity architecture, security requirements, network constraints, and deployment model tradeoffs

 - Partner with Deployment Strategists to turn customer goals into implementation plans that are aligned with the account strategy and grounded in technical reality

 - Build and configure AI-powered workflows across IT, HR, finance, and operations, adapting Console to each customer’s environment

 - Debug ambiguous customer issues, diagnose problems across product, integration, infrastructure, and configuration layers, and drive them to resolution

 - Ship fixes, product changes, scripts, or technical workarounds when needed to unblock strategic customers

 - Act as the technical advisor customers trust when they need to understand what is possible, what will scale, and what tradeoffs they should make

 - Bring field learnings back to Product and Engineering: edge cases, deployment patterns, technical limitations, and customer needs that should shape the roadmap




WHO YOU ARE

 - 3+ years of experience in a role where you wrote code, shipped production systems, and worked directly with customers to solve complex problems

 - You’re deeply technical and comfortable reasoning through systems, integrations, APIs, identity, networking, infrastructure, and AI product behavior

 - You are AI-native: you actively use AI tools, understand how they change workflows, and have strong instincts for where AI products work well or break down

 - You can move fluidly between customer conversations, technical investigation, workflow design, and hands-on implementation

 - You have strong customer judgment and can explain technical tradeoffs clearly to operators, technical teams, and executives

 - You take ownership of hard, ambiguous problems and keep digging until you understand what i

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.





ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Platform Engineer at Console, you’ll build the systems that make our engineering team faster and our product safer to change. You’ll own the build tooling, CI/CD, verification environments, release systems, and reusable product primitives that let us ship quickly without losing quality as the company scales.



This role starts with traditional platform engineering: faster builds, better local development, safer deploys, stronger test infrastructure, and cleaner shared architecture. It extends into the systems we use to ship, verify, and release changes in a product we intend to operate increasingly autonomously, including customer bug triage, validation, and resolution loops.



You’ll have room to operate autonomously, work closely with the CTO and engineering team, and shape how a modern AI-native engineering organization should build software. The best people in this role care deeply about developer experience, release quality, product architecture, and the compounding leverage that comes from making every engineer faster.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the internal platform, build tooling, and CI/CD workflows that move code from idea to production

 - Make builds faster, tests more reliable, deployments safer, and releases easier to understand and operate

 - Design and operate verification environments that validate product behavior across agents, integrations, workflows, and customer-like scenarios before changes reach production

 - Build AI-native tooling for automated development, bug triage, validation, release management, and operational feedback loops

 - Create the systems that help Console detect issues, reason about changes, verify fixes, and eventually automate more of the path from customer bug report to production resolution

 - Build reusable product primitives and architecture patterns that help teams ship new agent capabilities, integrations, and context-aware workflows without reinventing core systems

 - Improve the day-to-day developer experience across local development, CI, testing, review, deployment, and debugging

 - Partner with product and engineering teams to identify repeated friction, turn it into platform leverage, and raise the quality bar across the codebase




WHO YOU ARE

 - 5+ years of full-time experience in backend, platform, product infrastructure, or developer tooling engineering

 - You have strong engineering fundamentals and are comfortable working across a modern TypeScript/Node/React codebase

 - You have built internal platforms, build systems, CI/CD workflows, developer tools, product infrastructure, or shared architecture that other engineers depend on

 - You are AI-native: you actively use AI tools, understand how they change software development, and have strong instincts for where they can compress engineering cycles

 - You have strong product taste for developer experience and care about building tools that engineers actually want to use

 - You can turn repeated engineering friction into reusable abstractions, better workflows

source_jobs.title: Product Lead
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.



The Role

We've found product-market fit building support automation for IT teams and that's just the beginning. We're hiring a Product Lead to help us achieve our broader vision: building the definitive operating system for operations teams.



You'll work closely with our Head of Product, design, and engineering to own product areas end-to-end and help define how great products get built at Console.



What you’ll do

Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle for one or more product areas, from strategy through shipping

 - Deeply understand our customers and the market, synthesizing signal into decisions that move the product forward

 - Solve multi-faceted product challenges that require innovation across AI, UX, and business models

 - Partner with engineering and design to ship high-quality product at pace

 - Help shape the product culture at Console and push the boundaries of what it means to build product in the age of AI



About you

 - 5+ years of experience in product management launching and scaling technical products; founder background and/or B2B SaaS experience is a plus

 - Strong technical background with experience working cross-functionally with engineering teams to ship complex products

 - Deeply curious with a growth mindset - the current rate of change in AI and software is exciting to you and you want to be in the thick of it

 - Great taste - you have strong opinions about how software should work and sweat the details

 - A natural leader who earns the trust of everyone around them, from engineering and design to customers and leadership

 - Equally comfortable urgently troubleshooting a P0 bug and developing a product strategy for entering a new market

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits including:
   
   - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
   
   - Unlimited PTO
   
   - 401(k)
   
   - Equity
   
   - Meals provided daily in office

source_jobs.title: Sr. Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.




THE ROLE

You’ll be the recruiting partner for Console’s engineering and applied AI teams. Working alongside our founding recruiter and directly with eng leaders, you’ll own the full lifecycle from sourcing through close across software engineering, infrastructure, AI/ML, forward deployed engineering, and security.



Hands-on and strategic. You’ll own your pipelines end-to-end, raise the bar on how we evaluate technical talent, and have real latitude to shape how eng recruiting works here.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Run full-cycle recruiting for engineering and AI roles, from pipeline to close, in a market where the best candidates have five other offers

 - Partner with hiring managers to define what “great” looks like for each role and hold a high bar across every hire

 - Get deep into our stack and product so you can talk shop with candidates and assess fit with real nuance

 - Source, engage, and close candidates directly, driving the process from first outreach to signed offer

 - Work with our founding recruiter and eng leaders to sharpen interview loops and level up how we evaluate talent

 - Make every candidate touchpoint reflect who we are: fast, thoughtful, and respectful of people’s time




WHAT YOU ARE

 - 4+ years full-cycle recruiting with a track record of hiring engineers, ideally at startups or high-growth companies

 - You know how to evaluate engineers across disciplines (backend, infra, AI/ML) and can calibrate with eng leaders on close calls

 - Strong sourcer who knows how to cut through the noise and engage candidates who aren’t actively looking

 - Experience improving interview processes and evaluation frameworks, not just filling reqs but making the recruiting org better

 - Comfortable with ambiguity, shifting priorities, and figuring things out without a playbook

 - Clear, sharp communicator across writing, calls, and debriefs




EVEN BETTER

 - You’ve hired for AI/ML or infrastructure roles and understand what separates good from great in those disciplines

 - You’ve been an early recruiting hire at a company that scaled and know what it takes to build the machine, not just run it

 - You’ve designed technical interview processes or evaluation frameworks from the ground up

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits including:
   
   - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
   
   - Unlimited PTO
   
   - 401(k)
   
   - Equity
   
   - Meals provided daily in office

The Role

As Console’s first GTM recruiting hire, you’ll build the playbook for how we attract and close go-to-market talent. Working alongside our founding recruiter and directly with GTM leaders, you’ll own the full lifecycle from sourcing through close across sales, marketing, growth, partnerships, and operations.



Hands-on and strategic. You’ll own your pipelines end-to-end across every GTM function, and your hiring decisions will directly shape what these teams look like as we scale.

 - Run full-cycle recruiting for GTM roles, from pipeline to close, in a market where strong candidates move fast

 - Partner with hiring managers to define what “great” looks like for each role and hold a high bar across every hire

 - Source, engage, and close candidates directly, driving the process from first outreach to signed offer

 - 4+ years full-cycle recruiting with a track record of hiring across GTM disciplines (sales, marketing, growth, ops), ideally at startups or high-growth companies

 - Strong sourcer who knows how to cut through the noise and engage candidates who aren’t actively looking

 - Experience improving interview processes and evaluation frameworks, not just filling reqs but making the recruiting org better

 - Comfortable with ambiguity, shifting priorities, and figuring things out without a playbook

 - Clear, sharp communicator across writing, calls, and debriefs



Even Better

 - You’ve hired across multiple GTM functions at once and know how to context-switch between a brand designer and a sales leader in the same week

 - You’ve designed GTM interview processes or evaluation frameworks from the ground up

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

source_jobs.title: Demand Gen Lead
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.




THE ROLE

As Console's Demand Gen Lead, you'll build the system that fills the top of our funnel and keeps it moving. You'll define the strategy, run the experiments, and turn what works into repeatable pipeline. You'll own paid and organic demand channels, design nurture campaigns, and work closely with Sales to make sure leads are worked.

This role is for someone who has helped build demand gen before, maybe as a founder, maybe as an early marketing hire, or maybe as someone who has started leading a small team and wants to scale that experience further. You love building from zero and get genuinely excited about a funnel that converts.

If you've worn the demand gen hat before and want to own both strategy and execution while helping shape the future of the function, this is your next move.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own full-funnel growth: Plan, launch, and optimize campaigns across awareness, demand gen, and pipeline acceleration.

 - Run rapid experimentation: Develop and own a structured testing engine across new channels, messaging, creative, landing pages, and funnel improvements.

 - Drive measurable pipeline: Build high-quality leads and opportunities across paid and non-paid channels, optimizing for CAC, conversion, and velocity.

 - Analyze and report impact: Surface insights, build hypotheses, and make data-backed recommendations to guide strategy.

 - Partner closely with Sales and GTM leadership: Ensure leads are worked effectively and feedback loops between Marketing and Sales stay tight.

 - Help build and mentor the demand gen function over time, including the opportunity to manage and grow a small team as the company scales.


ABOUT YOU

 - 4-8 years in B2B SaaS growth, demand gen, or performance marketing (early-stage and high-growth strongly preferred).

 - Proven ability to design and ship full-funnel campaigns across multiple channels.

 - Strong analytical rigor: comfortable pulling data, interpreting funnel performance, and making fast decisions.

 - Creative problem-solver who can generate test ideas and turn successful experiments into scalable playbooks.

 - High ownership, comfortable with ambiguity, and motivated by impact over process.

 - Experience with attribution, funnel instrumentation, and experimentation frameworks (GA4, GTM, SQL a plus).

 - Prior people management experience is a plus, but not required. We’re especially interested in candidates who have demonstrated strong ownership, mentorship, and leadership potential.

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend to grow talent from within as we scale up.

 - Competitive pay and benefits: top compensation with full benefits includi

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Deployment Strategist at Console, you’ll own the success of our largest enterprise customers. You’ll partner with executives and operating teams to turn complex, cross-functional workflows into high-impact AI deployments, then stay close to the account as adoption expands across the organization.

This is a high-ownership role at the intersection of consulting, project leadership, customer strategy, and AI deployment. You’ll lead a portfolio of strategic accounts, build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders, and own the plan that will drive them from initial deployment to a measurable business impact. To do this, you'll work closely with FDEs, Product, Engineering, and Sales but you’ll be responsible for the account’s operating rhythm: making sure the right people are aligned, agents are working correctly, and Console’s footprint expands over time.

In this role, you’ll have room to operate autonomously, shape how Fortune 500 customers deploy AI across their organizations, and define what great enterprise customer ownership looks like at Console.






WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own a portfolio of strategic enterprise accounts, serving as the primary relationship owner and trusted advisor to customer leadership

 - Drive complex implementations from kickoff to launch, building project plans which turn ambiguous enterprise goals into clear work streams, owners, timelines, and success metrics

 - Translate messy business processes across IT, HR, finance, and operations into clear deployment priorities and expansion opportunities

 - Partner with FDEs to connect customer goals to the right technical implementation, ensuring deployments are both operationally successful and technically sound

 - Lead executive check-ins, working sessions, and cross-functional customer meetings with clarity, urgency, and strong follow-through

 - Identify risks early, unblock stalled work, and bring structure to ambiguous customer environments

 - Track adoption, ROI, and automation coverage across accounts, using those signals to drive long-term success and expansion

 - Bring enterprise customer learnings back to Product, Engineering, and Sales: deployment patterns, stakeholder dynamics, process gaps, and unmet needs that shape the platform






WHO YOU ARE

 - 3+ years of experience in consulting, enterprise customer success, implementation, strategy and operations, or another role where you owned complex projects with senior stakeholders

 - You’re an exceptional problem solver who can quickly understand a customer’s business, identify what matters, and create a practical path to impact

 - You have strong project management instincts: you create clarity, manage competing priorities, hold stakeholders accountable, and keep momentum through ambiguity

 - You communicate with precision and presence, whether you’re facilitating a working session with operators or presenting an implementation plan to executives

 - You’re technically fluent enough to reason through systems, workflows, integrations, and AI

source_jobs.title: AI Customer Enablement Lead
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.


THE ROLE

We're looking for an AI Customer Enablement Lead to own how customers learn, adopt, and get the most value from Console. This is a founding seat: you'll architect our enterprise enablement function from the ground up, across onboarding, product training, self-serve resources, documentation, and ongoing enablement.

What you build is going to be rolling out to enterprise customers like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor, directly shaping adoption, retention, and how they scale. You'll partner closely with Forward Deployed Engineering, Product, Sales, and Engineering to turn complex capabilities into clear learning experiences, with the autonomy to build fast, iterate, and own programs end-to-end.

The ideal candidate brings strong instructional design instincts and a track record of scalable enablement programs in B2B SaaS.



What You'll Do

 - Own Console's enterprise enablement strategy and roadmap, setting the direction as the function takes shape.

 - Architect the function end-to-end (content, tooling, delivery, metrics) and build the case for where it scales next, which may include growing a team over time.

 - Build onboarding and training resources that help customers adopt AI agents fast, including guides, videos, courses, and help center content.

 - Design scalable learning experiences across customer personas, use cases, and stages of the journey, from first deployment to mature, multi-team usage.

 - Partner with Forward Deployed Engineering, Product, Sales, and Engineering to surface customer knowledge gaps and turn them into high-impact enablement.

 - Build repeatable systems for gathering feedback, measuring content effectiveness, and improving programs over time.



About You

 - 6+ years in customer enablement or education, including ownership of a program or function, ideally in B2B SaaS or enterprise technology.

 - A track record of building enablement programs from zero: onboarding resources, academies, training content, or self-serve learning. You think about content as building blocks of a larger system.

 - Strong ability to translate complex or technical products into clear, approachable learning experiences. Comfort with AI or highly technical products is a plus.

 - Fluent across formats: documentation, training decks, videos, webinars, live trainings, and self-paced learning.

 - A high-agency operator who creates structure in ambiguity and is excited to build zero to one.

 - Comfortable operating with senior stakeholders on both sides: setting strategy internally with leadership and engaging enterprise customers at the VP and executive level.

Why join Console?

 - Product-market fit: We have built the leading product in our category, in a massive market. We've hit an inflection point and are on track to build a generational company.

 - World-class team: We seek high agency contributors who are comfortable navigating ambiguity, ruthlessly prioritize what matters and are action-biased.

 - Grow with us: We reward impact, not credentials or years of experience. We intend

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Console is building the AI agents that power autonomous enterprises. We use AI to autonomously resolve IT, HR, Legal, Finance, Security, and Ops tasks the moment they come in from Slack or Teams. We're building toward a future where enterprise operations are fully autonomous; with no tickets, approvals, or manual processes slowing companies down.



Our platform enables companies like Databricks, Figma, and Cursor to delegate entire categories of work to AI agents that understands their org, takes action across systems, and gets smarter over time. We've raised $29M, backed by Thrive Capital (investors in OpenAI, Stripe, GitHub) and a small group of founders who have built category-defining companies.



We’re unusually selective about the people we work with. We hire builders who span functions, optimize for speed, and care deeply about their craft. We do not care about pedigree. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Join us.





ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Infrastructure Engineer at Console, you’ll build the foundation that lets Console run reliably for the world’s largest enterprises. You’ll own the cloud infrastructure, deployment models, and core runtime systems that need to scale as customers trust Console with more of their operational workflows.



This role sits close to the hardest parts of the product: making our cloud-hosted platform enterprise-grade, scaling the graph and search infrastructure behind Console’s context layer, and building a self-hosted deployment model that works for customers with strict security, data, and infrastructure constraints. You’ll make foundational architecture decisions across reliability, performance, cost, isolation, and operability.



You’ll work across product engineering, platform, security, and customer-facing teams to make Console deployable in the environments our customers actually run. The best people in this role are infrastructure builders who want to own real systems end-to-end: from architecture and implementation to production operations and enterprise deployment.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and operate the infrastructure behind Console’s cloud-hosted platform across AWS, Kubernetes, and related cloud systems, making critical tradeoffs across performance, cost, reliability, and security

 - Architect and ship a scalable self-hosted version of Console for enterprise customers with strict data, security, and deployment requirements

 - Own core infrastructure components that need to scale with customer complexity, including the graph, search, queueing, and runtime systems behind Console’s context layer

 - Design deployment patterns that let Console run reliably across hosted and customer-hosted environments without slowing down product velocity

 - Partner with customer-facing teams on enterprise deployment requirements, translating real customer constraints into durable infrastructure improvements

 - Improve observability, reliability, incident response, and operational tooling as Console grows from early-scale to enterprise-grade

 - Make foundational architecture decisions that help the engineering team ship faster without compromising the stability customers depend on




WHO YOU ARE

 - 5+ years of full-time experience in infrastructure, backend, platform, or distributed systems engineering

 - You have strong hands-on experience with AWS, Kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure, with a real understanding of the tradeoffs behind architecture decisions

 - You have experience with IaC tools like Pulumi or Terraform and are comfortable working in a modern TypeScript/Node/React codebase

 - You’ve built and operated production-grade systems where reliability, scale, security, and observability mattered

 - You can reason through deployment models, networking, data isolation, infrastructure constraints, and operational failure modes

 - You take ownership of hard systems problems and keep digging until the underlying architecture is correct

 - You care deeply about craft, reliab

notes: High-tech startup with strong focus on AI and autonomous enterprise operations
